title: Cursor vs Windsurf vs Copilot: which AI IDE actually ships faster?
post:
I shipped the same project in all three AI IDEs. Here's what I found:

Cursor:
Fastest for feature development. Tab completion is genuinely magical. The agent mode understands project context better than anything I've tried. Minor learning curve, major productivity gain.

Windsurf:
Best for exploring new codebases. The waterfall feature shows its work in a way that builds trust. Good for onboarding to unfamiliar projects.

Copilot:
Most integrated. If you're already in VS Code, it Just Works. Not the most sophisticated, but the frictionless integration wins for daily use.

My verdict: Cursor for serious development velocity, Copilot for convenience, Windsurf for learning new codebases.

The productivity difference between these and plain VS Code is not subtle. It's night and day.

Which AI IDE are you shipping with?
